info.title: Irodori-TTS Server
info.summary: TTS API serving Irodori-TTS speaker LoRAs over HTTP.
info.description:
FastAPI front-end for Irodori-TTS speaker LoRAs.

One base checkpoint is loaded once at startup; every ``.safetensors`` LoRA
discovered under ``lora_dir`` is registered as a speaker and swapped in
per-request. Speaker identity is carried entirely by the LoRA — the server
runs with ``no_ref=true`` and does not accept reference waveforms.

## Typical flow

1. ``GET /health`` — liveness probe and speaker count.
2. ``GET /speakers`` — list registered speakers and their UUIDs.
3. ``POST /synth`` — submit ``{speaker_id, text, ...}`` and receive an
   ``audio/wav`` body. Sampling knobs are optional; unspecified fields fall
   back to per-LoRA defaults and then to built-in defaults.

## Response headers on ``/synth``

| Header                 | Meaning                                            |
|------------------------|----------------------------------------------------|
| ``X-TTS-Speaker-Id``   | Echo of the resolved speaker UUID.                 |
| ``X-TTS-Speaker-Name`` | URL-encoded display name of the speaker.           |
| ``X-TTS-Used-Seed``    | Seed actually used (useful when ``seed`` is auto). |
| ``X-TTS-Sample-Rate``  | Sample rate of the returned WAV, in Hz.            |

## Output format

- Container: ``audio/wav`` (PCM16).
- Length: fixed 30-second window; the sampler trims tail silence but clients
  may still want to re-trim.
- Sample rate: determined by the DACVAE codec (reported via
  ``X-TTS-Sample-Rate``).

get.description: Returns ``{status: 'ok', speakers: <count>}`` once the process is up. Cheap — does not touch the GPU — safe to poll from load balancers or Discord bot health checks.

Returns every LoRA adapter discovered under ``lora_dir`` at startup, along with its stable UUID and the sampling defaults baked into the LoRA metadata. Clients should cache this list and re-fetch only when the server is restarted — new ``.safetensors`` files are **not** picked up at runtime.

post.summary: Synthesize speech for a registered speaker
post.description: Runs text-to-speech for ``speaker_id`` and streams the result as a WAV body. All sampling knobs are optional — omit them to get the speaker's trained defaults. The endpoint is synchronous and can take several seconds per request depending on ``num_steps`` and GPU load; callers should set a generous HTTP timeout (30–60s is typical).

200.description: Generated speech as PCM16 audio. Use ``Accept: audio/wav`` (default) for a WAV file or ``Accept: audio/pcm`` for a raw PCM16 mono stream. Inspect the ``X-TTS-*`` response headers for the resolved speaker, used seed, and sample rate.

X-TTS-Speaker-Id.description: Echo of the resolved speaker UUID.

X-TTS-Speaker-Name.description: URL-encoded display name of the speaker.

X-TTS-Sample-Rate.description: Sample rate of the returned WAV, in Hz.

404.description: ``speaker_id`` does not match any loaded LoRA.

500.description: Synthesis failed inside the model runtime.

503.description: No speakers are loaded. The server started without any LoRA under ``lora_dir``; add one and restart.

status.title: Status
status.description: Always ``ok`` when the process is live.

speakers.title: Speakers
speakers.description: Number of LoRA adapters loaded at startup.

uuid.title: Uuid
uuid.description: Stable speaker identifier. Use this value as ``speaker_id`` when calling ``POST /synth``. Derived from the LoRA ``.safetensors`` metadata (or, as a fallback, UUIDv5 of the file stem), so the same file always resolves to the same UUID across restarts.

name.title: Name
name.description: Human-readable display name embedded in the LoRA metadata.

defaults.title: Defaults
defaults.description: Per-speaker default sampling parameters baked into the LoRA metadata. Any field omitted from a ``/synth`` request falls back to these values, then to the server-wide defaults (``num_steps=40``, ``cfg_scale_text=3.0``, ``cfg_scale_speaker=5.0``).

speakers.title: Speakers
speakers.description: All LoRA adapters discovered at startup under ``lora_dir``.

speaker_id.title: Speaker Id
speaker_id.description: Target speaker UUID as returned by ``GET /speakers``. Unknown ids return HTTP 404.

text.title: Text
text.description: Text to synthesize. Japanese is the primary target. There is no hard upper bound enforced here, but the output is clamped to 30 seconds, so very long inputs will be truncated by the sampler.
examples: こんにちは、今日はいい天気ですね。

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: Sampling seed for reproducibility. Omit or pass a negative value for a random seed; the actually-used seed is echoed back in the ``X-TTS-Used-Seed`` response header.

num_steps.title: Num Steps
num_steps.description: Number of rectified-flow sampling steps. Higher = slower but usually cleaner. Omit or set ``<=0`` to use the speaker default (typically 40).

cfg_scale_text.title: Cfg Scale Text
cfg_scale_text.description: Classifier-free guidance scale for the text conditioning. Higher values make the output track the text more strictly at the cost of naturalness. Omit or set ``<=0`` to use the speaker default (~3.0).

cfg_scale_speaker.title: Cfg Scale Speaker
cfg_scale_speaker.description: CFG scale for the speaker conditioning. Higher values push harder toward the target speaker identity. Omit or set ``<=0`` to use the speaker default (~5.0).

speaker_kv_scale.title: Speaker Kv Scale
speaker_kv_scale.description: Extra KV-cache scaling applied to the speaker stream. Values ``>1`` strengthen speaker identity (useful when a LoRA sounds under-trained); omit or set ``<=0`` to disable.

truncation_factor.title: Truncation Factor
truncation_factor.description: Noise truncation factor (e.g. ``0.8``). Lower values reduce variance and tend to produce safer but less expressive samples. Omit or set ``<=0`` to disable.

SynthRequest.title: SynthRequest
SynthRequest.description:
Request body of ``POST /synth``.

Only ``speaker_id`` and ``text`` are required. Sampling knobs are optional:
omitting a field (or passing a non-positive value for the positive-only ones)
causes the server to fall back to the LoRA metadata ``defaults`` and then to
the built-in defaults. The server always runs with ``no_ref=true`` — speaker
identity comes from the LoRA, not from a reference waveform — and returns a
fixed 30-second WAV (tail silence is trimmed internally but clients may still
want to re-trim).

tags.name: speakers
tags.description: Speaker discovery — list LoRA adapters loaded at startup.
